type: ignore

class_variables.name: textual.widget.Widget.border_subtitle
documentation: A title to show in the bottom border (if there is one).
type: ignore

class_variables.name: textual.widget.Widget._PSEUDO_CLASSES
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

A Widget is the base class for Textual widgets.

See also [static][textual.widgets._static.Static] for starting point for your own widgets.

functions.name: textual.css.parse._add_specificity
documentation:
Add specificity tuples together.

Args:
    specificity1: Specificity triple.
    specificity2: Specificity triple.

Returns:
    Combined specificity.

functions.name: textual.css.parse.parse_declarations
documentation:
Parse declarations and return a Styles object.

Args:
    css: String containing CSS.
    read_from: The location where the CSS was read from.

Returns:
    A styles object.

functions.name: textual.css.parse._unresolved
documentation:
Raise a TokenError regarding an unresolved variable.

Args:
    variable_name: A variable name.
    variables: Possible choices used to generate suggestion.
    token: The Token.

Raises:
    UnresolvedVariableError: Always raises a TokenError.

functions.name: textual.css.parse.substitute_references
documentation:
Replace variable references with values by substituting variable reference
tokens with the tokens representing their values.

Args:
    tokens: Iterator of Tokens which may contain tokens
        with the name "variable_ref".

Returns:
    Yields Tokens such that any variable references (tokens where
        token.name == "variable_ref") have been replaced with the tokens representing
        the value. In other words, an Iterable of Tokens similar to the original input,
        but with variables resolved. Substituted tokens will have their referenced_by
        attribute populated with information about where the tokens are being substituted to.

functions.name: textual.css.parse.parse
documentation:
Parse CSS by tokenizing it, performing variable substitution,
and generating rule sets from it.

Args:
    scope: CSS type name.
    css: The input CSS.
    read_from: The source location of the CSS.
    variables: Substitution variables to substitute tokens for.
    is_default_rules: True if the rules we're extracting are
        default (i.e. in Widget.DEFAULT_CSS) rules. False if they're from user defined CSS.

modules.name: textual.message_pump
documentation:
A `MessagePump` is a base class for any object which processes messages, which includes Widget, Screen, and App.

!!! tip

    Most of the method here are useful in general app development.

classes.name: textual.message_pump._MessagePumpMeta
documentation:
Metaclass for message pump. This exists to populate a Message inner class of a Widget with the
parent classes' name.

classes.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._prevent_message_types_stack
documentation: The stack that manages prevented messages.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._get_prevented_messages
documentation: A set of all the prevented message types.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._is_prevented
documentation:
Check if a message type has been prevented via the
[prevent][textual.message_pump.MessagePump.prevent] context manager.

Args:
    message_type: A message type.

Returns:
    `True` if the message has been prevented from sending, or `False` if it will be sent as normal.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.prevent
documentation:
A context manager to *temporarily* prevent the given message types from being posted.

Example:
    ```python
    input = self.query_one(Input)
    with self.prevent(Input.Changed):
        input.value = "foo"
    ```

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.has_parent
documentation: Does this object have a parent?

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.message_queue_size
documentation: The current size of the message queue.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.is_dom_root
documentation: Is this a root node (i.e. the App)?

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.app
documentation:
Get the current app.

Returns:
    The current app.

Raises:
    NoActiveAppError: if no active app could be found for the current asyncio context

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.is_attached
documentation: Is this node linked to the app through the DOM?

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.is_parent_active
documentation: Is the parent active?

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.is_running
documentation: Is the message pump running (potentially processing messages)?

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.log
documentation:
Get a logger for this object.

Returns:
    A logger.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._attach
documentation:
Set the parent, and therefore attach this node to the tree.

Args:
    parent: Parent node.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._detach
documentation: Set the parent to None to remove the node from the tree.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.check_message_enabled
documentation:
Check if a given message is enabled (allowed to be sent).

Args:
    message: A message object.

Returns:
    `True` if the message will be sent, or `False` if it is disabled.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.disable_messages
documentation: Disable message types from being processed.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.enable_messages
documentation: Enable processing of messages types.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._get_message
documentation:
Get the next event on the queue, or None if queue is closed.

Returns:
    Event object or None.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._peek_message
documentation:
Peek the message at the head of the queue (does not remove it from the queue),
or return None if the queue is empty.

Returns:
    The message or None.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.set_timer
documentation:
Call a function after a delay.

Example:
    ```python
    def ready():
        self.notify("Your soft boiled egg is ready!")
    # Call ready() after 3 minutes
    self.set_timer(3 * 60, ready)
    ```

Args:
    delay: Time (in seconds) to wait before invoking callback.
    callback: Callback to call after time has expired.
    name: Name of the timer (for debug).
    pause: Start timer paused.

Returns:
    A timer object.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.set_interval
documentation:
Call a function at periodic intervals.

Args:
    interval: Time (in seconds) between calls.
    callback: Function to call.
    name: Name of the timer object.
    repeat: Number of times to repeat the call or 0 for continuous.
    pause: Start the timer paused.

Returns:
    A timer object.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.call_after_refresh
documentation:
Schedule a callback to run after all messages are processed and the screen
has been refreshed. Positional and keyword arguments are passed to the callable.

Args:
    callback: A callable.

Returns:
    `True` if the callback was scheduled, or `False` if the callback could not be
        scheduled (may occur if the message pump was closed or closing).

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.call_later
documentation:
Schedule a callback to run after all messages are processed in this object.
Positional and keywords arguments are passed to the callable.

Args:
    callback: Callable to call next.
    *args: Positional arguments to pass to the callable.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments to pass to the callable.

Returns:
    `True` if the callback was scheduled, or `False` if the callback could not be
        scheduled (may occur if the message pump was closed or closing).

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.call_next
documentation:
Schedule a callback to run immediately after processing the current message.

Args:
    callback: Callable to run after current event.
    *args: Positional arguments to pass to the callable.
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments to pass to the callable.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._close_messages
documentation: Close message queue, and optionally wait for queue to finish processing.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._start_messages
documentation: Start messages task.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._message_loop_exit
documentation: Called when the message loop has completed.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._pre_process
documentation:
Procedure to run before processing messages.

Returns:
    `True` if successful, or `False` if any exception occurred.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._post_mount
documentation: Called after the object has been mounted.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._close_messages_no_wait
documentation: Request the message queue to immediately exit.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._context
documentation: Context manager to set ContextVars.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._process_messages_loop
documentation: Process messages until the queue is closed.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._flush_next_callbacks
documentation: Invoke pending callbacks in next callbacks queue.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._dispatch_message
documentation:
Dispatch a message received from the message queue.

Args:
    message: A message object

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._get_dispatch_methods
documentation:
Gets handlers from the MRO

Args:
    method_name: Handler method name.
    message: Message object.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.on_event
documentation:
Called to process an event.

Args:
    event: An Event object.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._on_message
documentation:
Called to process a message.

Args:
    message: A Message object.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.check_idle
documentation: Prompt the message pump to call idle if the queue is empty.

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump._post_message
documentation:
Post a message or an event to this message pump.

This is an internal method for use where a coroutine is required.

Args:
    message: A message object.

Returns:
    True if the messages was posted successfully, False if the message was not posted
        (because the message pump was in the process of closing).

methods.name: textual.message_pump.MessagePump.post_message
documentation:
Posts a message on to this widget's queue.

Args:
    message: A message (including Event).

Returns:
    `True` if the messages was processed, `False` if it wasn't.
Base class which supplies a message pump.

classes.name: textual.css.tokenizer.TokenError

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.TokenError.__init__

Args:
    read_from: The location where the CSS was read from.

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.TokenError._get_snippet

Get a short snippet of code around a given line number.

    A renderable.
Error raised when the CSS cannot be tokenized (syntax error).

classes.name: textual.css.tokenizer.EOFError

classes.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Expect

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Expect.__init__
documentation:
Create Expect object.

Args:
    description: Description of this class of tokens, used in errors.
Object that describes the format of tokens.

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Token.start
documentation: Start line and column (1-indexed).

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Token.end
documentation: End line and column (1-indexed).

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Token.with_reference
documentation:
Return a copy of the Token, with reference information attached.
This is used for variable substitution, where a variable reference
can refer to tokens which were defined elsewhere. With the additional
ReferencedBy data attached, we can track where the token we are referring
to is used.

class_variables.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Token.location
documentation: Token starting location, 0-indexed.

classes.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Tokenizer

methods.name: textual.css.tokenizer.Tokenizer.__init__
documentation:

modules.name: textual.css.query
documentation:
This module contains the `DOMQuery` class and related objects.

A DOMQuery is a set of DOM nodes returned by [query][textual.dom.DOMNode.query].

The set of nodes may be further refined with [filter][textual.css.query.DOMQuery.filter] and [exclude][textual.css.query.DOMQuery.exclude].
Additional methods apply actions to all nodes in the query.

!!! info

    If this sounds like JQuery, a (once) popular JS library, it is no coincidence.

variables.name: textual.css.query.QueryType
documentation: Type variable used to type generic queries.

variables.name: textual.css.query.ExpectType
documentation: Type variable used to further restrict queries.

classes.name: textual.css.query.QueryError
documentation: Base class for a query related error.

classes.name: textual.css.query.InvalidQueryFormat
documentation: Query did not parse correctly.

classes.name: textual.css.query.NoMatches
documentation: No nodes matched the query.

classes.name: textual.css.query.TooManyMatches
documentation: Too many nodes matched the query.

classes.name: textual.css.query.WrongType
documentation: Query result was not of the correct type.

methods.name: textual.css.query.DOMQuery.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a query object.

!!! warning

    You won't need to construct this manually, as `DOMQuery` objects are returned by [query][textual.dom.DOMNode.query].

Args:
    node: A DOM node.
    filter: Query to filter children in the node.
    exclude: Query to exclude children in the node.
    deep: Query should be deep, i.e. recursive.
    parent: The parent query, if this is the result of filtering another query.

Raises:
    InvalidQueryFormat: If the format of the query is invalid.

documentation: True if non-empty, otherwise False.

Get the *only* matching node.

Represents the definition location of a piece of CSS code.

variable from where the CSS was read (e.g., "Widget.DEFAULT_CSS"), otherwise it's an
empty string.

methods.name: textual.css._styles_builder.StylesBuilder._distribute_importance
documentation:
Distribute importance amongst all aspects of the given style.

Args:
    prefix: The prefix of the style.
    suffixes: The suffixes to distribute amongst.

A number of styles can be set with the 'prefix' of the style,
providing the values as a series of parameters; or they can be set
with specific suffixes. Think `border` vs `border-left`, etc. This
method is used to ensure that if the former is set, `!important` is
distributed amongst all the suffixes.

documentation:
Returns a valid CSS property "Python" name, or None if no close matches could be found.

Args:
    rule_name: An invalid "Python-ised" CSS property (i.e. "offst_x" rather than "offst-x")

Returns:
    The closest valid "Python-ised" CSS property.
        Returns `None` if no close matches could be found.

Example: returns "background" for rule_name "bkgrund", "offset_x" for "ofset_x"

The StylesBuilder object takes tokens parsed from the CSS and converts
to the appropriate internal types.

value).

Attributes:
    summary: A succinct summary of the issue.
    bullets: Bullet points which provide additional
        context around the issue. These are rendered below the summary.

functions.name: textual._dispatch_key.dispatch_key
documentation:
Dispatch a key event to method.

This function will call the method named 'key_<event.key>' on a node if it exists.
Some keys have aliases. The first alias found will be invoked if it exists.
If multiple handlers exist that match the key, an exception is raised.

Args:
    event: A key event.

Returns:
    True if key was handled, otherwise False.

Raises:
    DuplicateKeyHandlers: When there's more than 1 handler that could handle this key.

variables.name: textual._types.CallbackType

variables.name: textual._types.IgnoreReturnCallbackType

variables.name: textual._types.WatchCallbackBothValuesType
documentation: Type for watch methods that accept the old and new values of reactive objects.

variables.name: textual._types.WatchCallbackNewValueType
documentation: Type for watch methods that accept only the new value of reactive objects.

variables.name: textual._types.WatchCallbackNoArgsType
documentation: Type for watch methods that do not require the explicit value of the reactive.

variables.name: textual._types.WatchCallbackType
documentation: Type used for callbacks passed to the `watch` method of widgets.

variables.name: textual._types.AnimationLevel

classes.name: textual._types.MessageTarget

classes.name: textual._types.UnusedParameter
documentation: Helper type for a parameter that isn't specified in a method call.

classes.name: textual.css.stylesheet.StylesheetParseError

documentation:
Contains the CSS content and whether or not the CSS comes from user defined stylesheets
vs widget-level stylesheets.

Args:
    content: The CSS as a string.
    is_defaults: True if the CSS is default (i.e. that defined at the widget level).
        False if it's user CSS (which will override the defaults).
    tie_breaker: Specificity tie breaker.
    scope: Scope of CSS.

classes.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet

List of rule sets.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.rules_map
documentation:
Structure that maps a selector on to a list of rules.

Returns:
    Mapping of selector to rule sets.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.css
documentation:
The equivalent TCSS for this stylesheet.

Note that this may not produce the same content as the file(s) used to generate the stylesheet.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of this stylesheet.

Returns:
    New stylesheet.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.set_variables
documentation:
Set CSS variables.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet._parse_rules
documentation:
Parse CSS and return rules.

Args:
    css: String containing Textual CSS.
    read_from: Original CSS location.
    is_default_rules: True if the rules we're extracting are
        default (i.e. in Widget.DEFAULT_CSS) rules. False if they're from user defined CSS.
    scope: Scope of rules, or empty string for global scope.

Raises:
    StylesheetError: If the CSS is invalid.

Returns:
    List of RuleSets.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.read
documentation:
Read Textual CSS file.

Args:
    filename: Filename of CSS.

Raises:
    StylesheetError: If the CSS could not be read.
    StylesheetParseError: If the CSS is invalid.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.read_all
documentation:
Read multiple CSS files, in order.

Args:
    paths: The paths of the CSS files to read, in order.

Raises:
    StylesheetError: If the CSS could not be read.
    StylesheetParseError: If the CSS is invalid.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.has_source
documentation:
Check if the stylesheet has this CSS source already.

Args:
    path: The file path of the source in question.
    class_var: The widget class variable we might be reading the CSS from.

Returns:
    Whether the stylesheet is aware of this CSS source or not.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.add_source
documentation:
Parse CSS from a string.

Args:
    css: String with CSS source.
    read_from: The original source location of the CSS.
    path: The path of the source if a file, or some other identifier.
    is_default_css: True if the CSS is defined in the Widget, False if the CSS is defined
        in a user stylesheet.
    tie_breaker: Integer representing the priority of this source.
    scope: CSS type name to limit scope or empty string for no scope.

Raises:
    StylesheetError: If the CSS could not be read.
    StylesheetParseError: If the CSS is invalid.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.parse
documentation:
Parse the source in the stylesheet.

Raises:
    StylesheetParseError: If there are any CSS related errors.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.reparse
documentation:
Re-parse source, applying new variables.

Raises:
    StylesheetError: If the CSS could not be read.
    StylesheetParseError: If the CSS is invalid.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet._check_rule
documentation:
Check a rule set, return specificity of applicable rules.

Args:
    rule_set: A rule set.
    css_path_nodes: A list of the nodes from the App to the node being checked.

Yields:
    Specificity of any matching selectors.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.apply
documentation:
Apply the stylesheet to a DOM node.

Args:
    node: The `DOMNode` to apply the stylesheet to.
        Applies the styles defined in this `Stylesheet` to the node.
        If the same rule is defined multiple times for the node (e.g. multiple
        classes modifying the same CSS property), then only the most specific
        rule will be applied.
    animate: Animate changed rules.
    cache: An optional cache when applying a group of nodes.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet._process_component_classes
documentation:
Process component classes for the given node.

Args:
    node: A DOM Node.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.replace_rules
documentation:
Replace style rules on a node, animating as required.

Args:
    node: A DOM node.
    rules: Mapping of rules.
    animate: Enable animation.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.update
documentation:
Update styles on node and its children.

Args:
    root: Root note to update.
    animate: Enable CSS animation.

methods.name: textual.css.stylesheet.Stylesheet.update_nodes
documentation:
Update styles for nodes.

Args:
    nodes: Nodes to update.
    animate: Enable CSS animation.
A Stylesheet generated from Textual CSS.

functions.name: textual._resolve.resolve
documentation:
Resolve a list of dimensions.

Args:
    dimensions: Scalars for column / row sizes.
    total: Total space to divide.
    gutter: Gutter between rows / columns.
    size: Size of container.
    viewport: Size of viewport.

Returns:
    List of (<OFFSET>, <LENGTH>)

functions.name: textual._resolve.resolve_fraction_unit
documentation:
Calculate the fraction.

Args:
    widget_styles: Styles for widgets with fraction units.
    size: Container size.
    viewport_size: Viewport size.
    remaining_space: Remaining space for fr units.
    resolve_dimension: Which dimension to resolve.

Returns:
    The value of 1fr.

functions.name: textual._resolve.resolve_box_models
documentation:
Resolve box models for a list of dimensions

Args:
    dimensions: A list of Scalars or Nones for each dimension.
    widgets: Widgets in resolve.
    size: Size of container.
    viewport_size: Viewport size.
    margin: Total space occupied by margin
    resolve_dimension: Which dimension to resolve.

Returns:
    List of resolved box models.

functions.name: textual._partition.partition
documentation:
Partition a sequence in to two list from a given predicate. The first list will contain
the values where the predicate is False, the second list will contain the remaining values.

Args:
    predicate: A callable that returns True or False for a given value.
    iterable: In Iterable of values.

Returns:
    A list of values where the predicate is False, and a list
        where the predicate is True.

variables.name: textual._animator.AnimationKey
documentation: Animation keys are the id of the object and the attribute being animated.

variables.name: textual._animator.EasingFunction
documentation:
Signature for a function that parametrizes animation speed.

An easing function must map the interval [0, 1] into the interval [0, 1].

classes.name: textual._animator.AnimationError
documentation: An issue prevented animation from starting.

classes.name: textual._animator.Animatable
documentation:
Protocol for objects that can have their intrinsic values animated.

For example, the transition between two colors can be animated
because the class [`Color`][textual.color.Color.blend] satisfies this protocol.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animation.__call__
documentation:
Call the animation, return a boolean indicating whether animation is in-progress or complete.

Args:
    time: The current timestamp

Returns:
    True if the animation has finished, otherwise False.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animation.invoke_callback
documentation: Calls the [`on_complete`][Animation.on_complete] callback if one is provided.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animation.stop
documentation:
Stop the animation.

Args:
    complete: Flag to say if the animation should be taken to completion.

class_variables.name: textual._animator.Animation.on_complete
documentation: Callback to run after animation completes

methods.name: textual._animator.SimpleAnimation.stop
documentation:
Stop the animation.

Args:
    complete: Flag to say if the animation should be taken to completion.

Note:
    [`on_complete`][Animation.on_complete] will be called regardless
    of the value provided for `complete`.

class_variables.name: textual._animator.SimpleAnimation.level
documentation: Minimum level required for the animation to take place (inclusive).

methods.name: textual._animator.BoundAnimator.__call__
documentation:
Animate an attribute.

Args:
    attribute: Name of the attribute to animate.
    value: The value to animate to.
    final_value: The final value of the animation. Defaults to `value` if not set.
    duration: The duration (in seconds) of the animation.
    speed: The speed of the animation.
    delay: A delay (in seconds) before the animation starts.
    easing: An easing method.
    on_complete: A callable to invoke when the animation is finished.
    level: Minimum level required for the animation to take place (inclusive).

classes.name: textual._animator.Animator

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the animator object.

Args:
    app: The application that owns the animator.
    frames_per_second: The number of frames/second to run the animation at.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.start
documentation: Start the animator task.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.stop
documentation: Stop the animator task.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.bind
documentation:
Bind the animator to a given object.

Args:
    obj: The object to bind to.

Returns:
    The bound animator.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.is_being_animated
documentation:
Does the object/attribute pair have an ongoing or scheduled animation?

Args:
    obj: An object to check for.
    attribute: The attribute on the object to test for.

Returns:
    `True` if that attribute is being animated for that object, `False` if not.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.animate
documentation:
Animate an attribute to a new value.

Args:

: obj: The object containing the attribute.
  attribute: The name of the attribute.
  value: The destination value of the attribute.
  final_value: The final value, or ellipsis if it is the same as `value`.
  duration: The duration of the animation, or `None` to use speed.
  speed: The speed of the animation.
  easing: An easing function.
  delay: Number of seconds to delay the start of the animation by.
  on_complete: Callback to run after the animation completes.
  level: Minimum level required for the animation to take place (inclusive).

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator._record_animation
documentation:
Called when an attribute is to be animated.

Args:
    attribute: Attribute being animated.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator._animate
documentation:
Animate an attribute to a new value.

Args:

: obj: The object containing the attribute.
  attribute: The name of the attribute.
  value: The destination value of the attribute.
  final_value: The final value, or ellipsis if it is the same as `value`.
  duration: The duration of the animation, or `None` to use speed.
  speed: The speed of the animation.
  easing: An easing function.
  on_complete: Callback to run after the animation completes.
  level: Minimum level required for the animation to take place (inclusive).

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator._stop_scheduled_animation
documentation:
Stop a scheduled animation.

Args:
    key: The key for the animation to stop.
    complete: Should the animation be moved to its completed state?

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator._stop_running_animation
documentation:
Stop a running animation.

Args:
    key: The key for the animation to stop.
    complete: Should the animation be moved to its completed state?

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.stop_animation
documentation:
Stop an animation on an attribute.

Args:
    obj: The object containing the attribute.
    attribute: The name of the attribute.
    complete: Should the animation be set to its final value?

Note:
    If there is no animation scheduled or running, this is a no-op.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.force_stop_animation
documentation:
Force stop an animation on an attribute. This will immediately stop the animation,
without running any associated callbacks, setting the attribute to its final value.

Args:
    obj: The object containing the attribute.
    attribute: The name of the attribute.

Note:
    If there is no animation scheduled or running, this is a no-op.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator._get_time
documentation:
Get the current wall clock time, via the internal Timer.

Returns:
    The wall clock time.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.wait_for_idle
documentation: Wait for any animations to complete.

methods.name: textual._animator.Animator.wait_until_complete
documentation: Wait for any current and scheduled animations to complete.
An object to manage updates to a given attribute over a period of time.

classes.name: textual.css.styles.RulesMap
documentation:
A typed dict for CSS rules.

Any key may be absent, indicating that rule has not been set.

Does not define composite rules, that is a rule that is made of a combination of other rules.

Get space around widget.

Removes the rule from the Styles object, as if it had never been set.

: rule_name: Rule name.

Returns:

: `True` if a rule was cleared, or `False` if the rule is already not set.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.get_rules

Get the rules in a mapping.

Set a rule.

  value: New rule value.

Returns:

: `True` if the rule changed, otherwise `False`.

Get an individual rule.

    rule_name: Name of rule.
    default: Default if rule does not exists.

Returns:
    Rule value or default.

documentation:
Mark the styles as requiring a refresh.

Args:
    layout: Also require a layout.
    children: Also refresh children.
    parent: Also refresh the parent.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.reset
documentation: Reset the rules to initial state.

documentation:
Merge values from another Styles.

Args:
    other: A Styles object.

Merge rules in to Styles.

Args:
    rules: A mapping of rules.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.get_render_rules
documentation: Get rules map with defaults.

documentation:
Check if a given rule may be animated.

Args:

: rule: Name of the rule.

Returns:

: `True` if the rule may be animated, otherwise `False`.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.parse
documentation:
Parse CSS and return a Styles object.

Args:
    css: Textual CSS.
    read_from: Location where the CSS was read from.
    node: Node to associate with the Styles.

Returns:
    A Styles instance containing result of parsing CSS.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase._get_transition
documentation:
Get a transition.

Args:
    key: Transition key.

Returns:
    Transition object or None it no transition exists.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase._align_width
documentation:
Align the width dimension.

Args:
    width: Width of the content.
    parent_width: Width of the parent container.

Returns:
    An offset to add to the X coordinate.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase._align_height
documentation:
Align the height dimensions

Args:
    height: Height of the content.
    parent_height: Height of the parent container.

Returns:
    An offset to add to the Y coordinate.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase._align_size
documentation:
Align a size according to alignment rules.

Args:
    child: The size of the child (width, height)
    parent: The size of the parent (width, height)

Returns:
    Offset required to align the child.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.partial_rich_style
documentation:
Get the style properties associated with this node only (not including parents in the DOM).

Returns:
    Rich Style object.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.display
documentation:
Set the display of the widget, defining how it's rendered.

Valid values are "block" or "none".

"none" will hide and allow other widgets to fill the space that this widget would occupy.

Set to None to clear any value that was set at runtime.

Raises:
    StyleValueError: If an invalid display is specified.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.visibility
documentation:
Set the visibility of the widget.

Valid values are "visible" or "hidden".

"hidden" will hide the widget, but reserve the space for this widget.
If you want to hide the widget and allow another widget to fill the space,
set the display attribute to "none" instead.

Set to None to clear any value that was set at runtime.

Raises:
    StyleValueError: If an invalid visibility is specified.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.layout
documentation:
Set the layout of the widget, defining how it's children are laid out.

Valid values are "grid", "horizontal", and "vertical" or None to clear any layout
that was set at runtime.

Raises:
    MissingLayout: If an invalid layout is specified.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.color
documentation:
Set the foreground (text) color of the widget.
Supports `Color` objects but also strings e.g. "red" or "#ff0000".
You can also specify an opacity after a color e.g. "blue 10%"

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.background
documentation:
Set the background color of the widget.
Supports `Color` objects but also strings e.g. "red" or "#ff0000"
You can also specify an opacity after a color e.g. "blue 10%"

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.background_tint
documentation:
Set a color to tint (blend) with the background.
Supports `Color` objects but also strings e.g. "red" or "#ff0000"
You can also specify an opacity after a color e.g. "blue 10%"

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.text_style
documentation:
Set the text style of the widget using Rich StyleFlags.
e.g. `"bold underline"` or `"b u strikethrough"`.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.opacity
documentation: Set the opacity of the widget, defining how it blends with the parent.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.text_opacity
documentation: Set the opacity of the content within the widget against the widget's background.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.padding
documentation: Set the padding (spacing between border and content) of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.margin
documentation: Set the margin (spacing outside the border) of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.offset
documentation: Set the offset of the widget relative to where it would have been otherwise.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.position
documentation: If `relative` offset is applied to widgets current position, if `absolute` it is applied to (0, 0).

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.border
documentation: Set the border of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.border_top
documentation: Set the top border of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.border_right
documentation: Set the right border of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.border_bottom
documentation: Set the bottom border of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.border_left
documentation: Set the left border of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.outline
documentation:
Set the outline of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".
The outline is drawn *on top* of the widget, rather than around it like border.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.outline_top
documentation: Set the top outline of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.outline_right
documentation: Set the right outline of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.outline_bottom
documentation: Set the bottom outline of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.outline_left
documentation: Set the left outline of the widget e.g. ("rounded", "green") or "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.width
documentation: Set the width of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.height
documentation: Set the height of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.min_width
documentation: Set the minimum width of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.min_height
documentation: Set the minimum height of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.max_width
documentation: Set the maximum width of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.max_height
documentation: Set the maximum height of the widget.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.dock
documentation: Set which edge of the parent to dock this widget to e.g. "top", "left", "right", "bottom", "none".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.overflow_x
documentation:
Control what happens when the content extends horizontally beyond the widget's width.

Valid values are "scroll", "hidden", or "auto".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.overflow_y
documentation:
Control what happens when the content extends vertically beyond the widget's height.

Valid values are "scroll", "hidden", or "auto".

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.tint
documentation:
Set the tint of the widget. This allows you apply a opaque color above the widget.

You can specify an opacity after a color e.g. "blue 10%"

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_color
documentation: Set the color of the handle of the scrollbar.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_color_hover
documentation: Set the color of the handle of the scrollbar when hovered.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_color_active
documentation: Set the color of the handle of the scrollbar when active (being dragged).

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_corner_color
documentation: Set the color of the space between the horizontal and vertical scrollbars.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_background
documentation: Set the background color of the scrollbar (the track that the handle sits on).

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_background_hover
documentation: Set the background color of the scrollbar when hovered.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_background_active
documentation: Set the background color of the scrollbar when active (being dragged).

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_gutter
documentation:
Set to "stable" to reserve space for the scrollbar even when it's not visible.
This can prevent content from shifting when a scrollbar appears.

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_size_vertical
documentation: Set the width of the vertical scrollbar (measured in cells).

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.scrollbar_size_horizontal
documentation: Set the height of the horizontal scrollbar (measured in cells).

class_variables.name: textual.css.styles.StylesBase.hatch
documentation: Add a hatched background effect e.g. ("right", "yellow") or "none" to use no hatch.
A common base class for Styles and RenderStyles

methods.name: textual.css.styles.Styles.copy
documentation: Get a copy of this Styles object.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.Styles.clear_rule
documentation:
Removes the rule from the Styles object, as if it had never been set.

Args:

: rule_name: Rule name.

Returns:

: `True` if a rule was cleared, or `False` if it was already not set.

methods.name: textual.css.styles.Styles.set_rule
documentation:
Set a rule.

Args:

: rule: Rule name.
  value: New rule value.

functions.name: textual._loop.loop_from_index
documentation:
Iterate over values in a sequence from a given starting index, potentially wrapping the index
if it would go out of bounds.

Note that the first value to be yielded is a step from `index`, and `index` will be yielded *last*.


Args:
    values: A sequence of values.
    index: Starting index.
    direction: Direction to move index (+1 for forward, -1 for backward).
    bool: Should the index wrap when out of bounds?

Yields:
    A tuple of index and value from the sequence.

documentation:
Initialize a Worker.

    node: The widget, screen, or App that initiated the work.

    name: Name of the worker (short string to help identify when debugging).
    group: The worker group.
    description: Description of the worker (longer string with more details).
    exit_on_error: Exit the app if the worker raises an error. Set to `False` to suppress exceptions.
    thread: Mark the worker as a thread worker.

documentation:
Progress as a percentage.

modules.name: textual.css._style_properties
documentation:
Style properties are descriptors which allow the `Styles` object to accept different types when
setting attributes. This gives the developer more freedom in how to express style information.

Descriptors also play nicely with Mypy, which is aware that attributes can have different types
when setting and getting.

classes.name: textual.css._style_properties.GenericProperty

methods.name: textual.css._style_properties.GenericProperty.validate_value
documentation:
Validate the setter value.

Args:
    value: The value being set.

Returns:
    The value to be set.

Descriptor that abstracts away common machinery for other style descriptors.

Args:
    default: The default value (or a factory thereof) of the property.
    layout: Whether to refresh the node layout on value change.
    refresh_children: Whether to refresh the node children on value change.

classes.name: textual.css._style_properties.BooleanProperty
documentation: A property that requires a True or False value.

Get the scalar property.

: obj: The `Styles` object.
  objtype: The `Styles` class.

Returns:

: The Scalar object or `None` if it's not set.

methods.name: textual.css._style_properties.ScalarProperty.__set__
documentation:
Set the scalar property.

Args:

: obj: The `Styles` object.
  value: The value to set the scalar property to.

  > You can directly pass a float or int value, which will be interpreted with
  > a default unit of Cells. You may also provide a string such as `"50%"`,
  > as you might do when writing CSS. If a string with no units is supplied,
  > Cells will be used as the unit. Alternatively, you can directly supply
  > a `Scalar` object.

Raises:
: StyleValueError: If the value is of an invalid type, uses an invalid unit, or

  : cannot be parsed for any other reason.
Descriptor for getting and setting scalar properties. Scalars are numeric values with a unit, e.g. "50vh".

Args:
    percent_unit: The dimension to which percentage scalars will be relative to.

methods.name: textual.css._style_properties.BoxProperty.__set__
documentation:
Set the box property.

Args:

: obj: The `Styles` object.
  value: A 2-tuple containing the type of box to use,

  > e.g. "dashed", and the `Style` to be used. You can supply the `Style` directly, or pass a
  > `str` (e.g. `"blue on #f0f0f0"` ) or `Color` instead.

Raises:

: StyleValueError: If the string supplied for the color is not a valid color.

Descriptor for getting and setting outlines and borders along a single edge.
For example "border-right", "outline-bottom", etc.

methods.name: textual.css._style_properties.BorderProperty.__set__
documentation:
Set the border.

Args:

: obj: The `Styles` object.
  border:

  > A `tuple[EdgeType, str | Color | Style]` representing the type of box to use and the `Style` to apply
  > to the box.
  > Alternatively, you can supply a sequence of these tuples and they will be applied per-edge.
  > If the sequence is of length 1, all edges will be decorated according to the single element.
  > If the sequence is length 2, the first `tuple` will be applied to the top and bottom edges.
  > If the sequence is length 4, the tuples will be applied to the edges in the order: top, right, bottom, left.

Raises:

: StyleValueError: When the supplied `tuple` is not of valid length (1, 2, or 4).

Descriptor for getting and setting full borders and outlines.

Args:
    layout: True if the layout should be refreshed after setting, False otherwise.

documentation:
Get the offset.

methods.name: textual.css._style_properties.OffsetProperty.__set__

type.name: tuple[int | str, int | str] | ScalarOffset | None

documentation:
Set the offset.

Args:

: obj: The `Styles` class.
  offset: A ScalarOffset object, or a 2-tuple of the form `(x, y)` indicating

  > the x and y offsets. When the `tuple` form is used, x and y can be specified
  > as either `int` or `str`. The string format allows you to also specify
  > any valid scalar unit e.g. `("0.5vw", "0.5vh")`.

Raises:
: ScalarParseError: If any of the string values supplied in the 2-tuple cannot

  : be parsed into a Scalar. For example, if you specify a non-existent unit.

Descriptor for getting and setting the offset property.
Offset consists of two values, x and y, that a widget's position
will be adjusted by before it is rendered.

inner_classes.name: textual.widgets._selection_list.SelectionList.SelectionHighlighted
documentation:
Message sent when a selection is highlighted.

Can be handled using `on_selection_list_selection_highlighted` in a subclass of
[`SelectionList`][textual.widgets.SelectionList] or in a parent node in the DOM.

inner_classes.name: textual.widgets._selection_list.SelectionList.SelectionToggled
documentation:
Message sent when a selection is toggled.

This is only sent when the value is *explicitly* toggled e.g.
via `toggle` or `toggle_all`, or via user interaction.
If you programmatically set a value to be selected, this message will
not be sent, even if it happens to be the opposite of what was
originally selected (i.e. setting a True to a False or vice-versa).

Since this message indicates a toggle occurring at a per-option level,
a message will be sent for each option that is toggled, even when a
bulk action is performed (e.g. via `toggle_all`).

Can be handled using `on_selection_list_selection_toggled` in a subclass of
[`SelectionList`][textual.widgets.SelectionList] or in a parent node in the DOM.

inner_classes.name: textual.widgets._selection_list.SelectionList.SelectedChanged

methods.name: textual.widgets._selection_list.SelectionList.SelectedChanged.control
documentation: An alias for `selection_list`.

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._selection_list.SelectionList.SelectedChanged.selection_list
documentation: The `SelectionList` that sent the message.

Message sent when the collection of selected values changes.

This is sent regardless of whether the change occurred via user interaction
or programmatically the the `SelectionList` API.

When a bulk change occurs, such as through `select_all` or `deselect_all`,
only a single `SelectedChanged` message will be sent (rather than one per
option).

Can be handled using `on_selection_list_selected_changed` in a subclass of
[`SelectionList`][textual.widgets.SelectionList] or in a parent node in the DOM.
A vertical selection list that allows making multiple selections.

class_variables.name: textual.command.CommandPalette.BINDINGS
documentation:
| Key(s) | Description |
| :- | :- |
| ctrl+end, shift+end | Jump to the last available commands. |
| ctrl+home, shift+home | Jump to the first available commands. |
| down | Navigate down through the available commands. |
| escape | Exit the command palette. |
| pagedown | Navigate down a page through the available commands. |
| pageup | Navigate up a page through the available commands. |
| up | Navigate up through the available commands. |

class_variables.name: textual.command.CommandPalette.run_on_select
documentation:
A flag to say if a command should be run when selected by the user.

If `True` then when a user hits `Enter` on a command match in the result
list, or if they click on one with the mouse, the command will be
selected and run. If set to `False` the input will be filled with the
command and then `Enter` should be pressed on the keyboard or the 'go'
button should be pressed.

methods.name: textual.widgets._input.Input.validate
documentation:
Run all the validators associated with this Input on the supplied value.

Runs all validators, combines the result into one. If any of the validators
failed, the combined result will be a failure. If no validators are present,
None will be returned. This also sets the `-invalid` CSS class on the Input
if the validation fails, and sets the `-valid` CSS class on the Input if
the validation succeeds.

Returns:
    A ValidationResult indicating whether *all* validators succeeded or not.
        That is, if *any* validator fails, the result will be an unsuccessful
        validation.

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._input.Input.BINDINGS
documentation:
| Key(s) | Description |
| :- | :- |
| left | Move the cursor left. |
| ctrl+left | Move the cursor one word to the left. |
| right | Move the cursor right or accept the completion suggestion. |
| ctrl+right | Move the cursor one word to the right. |
| backspace | Delete the character to the left of the cursor. |
| home,ctrl+a | Go to the beginning of the input. |
| end,ctrl+e | Go to the end of the input. |
| delete,ctrl+d | Delete the character to the right of the cursor. |
| enter | Submit the current value of the input. |
| ctrl+w | Delete the word to the left of the cursor. |
| ctrl+u | Delete everything to the left of the cursor. |
| ctrl+f | Delete the word to the right of the cursor. |
| ctrl+k | Delete everything to the right of the cursor. |

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._input.Input.COMPONENT_CLASSES
documentation:
| Class | Description |
| :- | :- |
| `input--cursor` | Target the cursor. |

| `input--suggestion` | Target the auto-completion suggestion (when it exists). |

methods.name: textual.widgets._placeholder.Placeholder._on_resize
documentation: Update the placeholder "size" variant with the new placeholder size.

A simple placeholder widget to use before you build your custom widgets.

This placeholder has a couple of variants that show different data.
Clicking the placeholder cycles through the available variants, but a placeholder
can also be initialised in a specific variant.

The variants available are:

| Variant | Placeholder shows                              |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|
| default | Identifier label or the ID of the placeholder. |
| size    | Size of the placeholder.                       |
| text    | Lorem Ipsum text.                              |

methods.name: textual.widgets._directory_tree.DirectoryTree._add_to_load_queue
documentation:
Add the given node to the load queue.

The return value can optionally be awaited until the queue is empty.

Args:
    node: The node to add to the load queue.

Returns:
    An optionally awaitable object that can be awaited until the
        load queue has finished processing.

documentation:
Reload the `DirectoryTree` contents.

Returns:

Clear all nodes under the given node.

documentation:
Clear the subtree and reset the given node.

Args:

    label: The label for the node.
    data: Optional data for the node.

Returns:
    The `Tree` instance.

methods.name: textual.widgets._directory_tree.DirectoryTree._reload
documentation:
Reloads the subtree rooted at the given node while preserving state.

After reloading the subtree, nodes that were expanded and still exist
will remain expanded and the highlighted node will be preserved, if it
still exists. If it doesn't, highlighting goes up to the first parent
directory that still exists.

Args:
    node: The root of the subtree to reload.

methods.name: textual.widgets._directory_tree.DirectoryTree.reload_node
documentation:
Reload the given node's contents.

The return value may be awaited to ensure the DirectoryTree has reached
a stable state and is no longer performing any node reloading (of this node
or any other nodes).

Args:
    node: The root of the subtree to reload.

Returns:
    An optionally awaitable that ensures the subtree has finished reloading.

methods.name: textual.widgets._directory_tree.DirectoryTree.validate_path

Ensure that the path is of the `Path` type.

Args:

Note:
    The result will always be a Python `Path` object, regardless of
    the value given.

methods.name: textual.widgets._directory_tree.DirectoryTree.watch_path
documentation:
Watch for changes to the `path` of the directory tree.

If the path is changed the directory tree will be repopulated using

methods.name: textual.widgets._tabbed_content.TabbedContent.add_pane
documentation:
Add a new pane to the tabbed content.

Args:
    pane: The pane to add.
    before: Optional pane or pane ID to add the pane before.
    after: Optional pane or pane ID to add the pane after.

Returns:
    An optionally awaitable object that waits for the pane to be added.

Raises:
    Tabs.TabError: If there is a problem with the addition request.

Note:
    Only one of `before` or `after` can be provided. If both are
    provided an exception is raised.

methods.name: textual.widgets._tabbed_content.TabbedContent.clear_panes

methods.name: textual.widgets._tabbed_content.TabbedContent._is_associated_tabs
documentation:
Determine whether a tab is associated with this TabbedContent or not.

A tab is "associated" with a `TabbedContent`, if it's one of the tabs that can
be used to control it. These have a special type: `ContentTab`, and are linked
back to this `TabbedContent` instance via a `tabbed_content` attribute.

Args:
    tabs: The Tabs instance to check.

Returns:
    True if the tab is associated with this `TabbedContent`.

| Key(s) | Description |

| :- | :- |

inner_classes.name: textual.widgets._switch.Switch.Changed

methods.name: textual.widgets._switch.Switch.Changed.control
documentation: Alias for self.switch.

Posted when the status of the switch changes.

Can be handled using `on_switch_changed` in a subclass of `Switch`
or in a parent widget in the DOM.

Attributes:
    value: The value that the switch was changed to.
    switch: The `Switch` widget that was changed.

A switch widget that represents a boolean value.

Can be toggled by clicking on it or through its [bindings][textual.widgets.Switch.BINDINGS].

The switch widget also contains [component classes][textual.widgets.Switch.COMPONENT_CLASSES]
that enable more customization.

    name: The name of the widget.
    id: The ID of the widget in the DOM.
    classes: The CSS classes of the widget.
    parser_factory: A factory function to return a configured MarkdownIt instance. If `None`, a "gfm-like" parser is used.

methods.name: textual.widgets._markdown.Markdown.sanitize_location
documentation:
Given a location, break out the path and any anchor.

Args:
    location: The location to sanitize.

Returns:
    A tuple of the path to the location cleaned of any anchor, plus
    the anchor (or an empty string if none was found).

methods.name: textual.widgets._markdown.Markdown.goto_anchor
documentation:
Try and find the given anchor in the current document.

Args:
    anchor: The anchor to try and find.

Note:
    The anchor is found by looking at all of the headings in the
    document and finding the first one whose slug matches the
    anchor.

    Note that the slugging method used is similar to that found on
    GitHub.

Returns:
    True when the anchor was found in the current document, False otherwise.

methods.name: textual.widgets._markdown.Markdown.load
documentation:
Load a new Markdown document.

Args:
    path: Path to the document.

Raises:
    OSError: If there was some form of error loading the document.

Note:
    The exceptions that can be raised by this method are all of
    those that can be raised by calling [`Path.read_text`][pathlib.Path.read_text].

methods.name: textual.widgets._markdown.Markdown.unhandled_token
documentation:
Process an unhandled token.

Args:
    token: The MarkdownIt token to handle.

Returns:
    Either a widget to be added to the output, or `None`.

methods.name: textual.widgets._markdown.Markdown.update

Args:

Returns:
    An optionally awaitable object. Await this to ensure that all children have been mounted.

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._markdown.Markdown.COMPONENT_CLASSES
documentation:
These component classes target standard inline markdown styles.
Changing these will potentially break the standard markdown formatting.

| Class | Description |
| :- | :- |
| `code_inline` | Target text that is styled as inline code. |
| `em` | Target text that is emphasized inline. |
| `s` | Target text that is styled inline with strikethrough. |
| `strong` | Target text that is styled inline with strong. |

classes.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.__init__
documentation:
Create a Progress Bar widget.

The progress bar uses "steps" as the measurement unit.

Example:
    ```py
    class MyApp(App):
        def compose(self):
            yield ProgressBar(total=100)

        def key_space(self):
            self.query_one(ProgressBar).advance(5)
    ```

Args:
    total: The total number of steps in the progress if known.
    show_bar: Whether to show the bar portion of the progress bar.
    show_percentage: Whether to show the percentage status of the bar.
    show_eta: Whether to show the ETA countdown of the progress bar.
    name: The name of the widget.
    id: The ID of the widget in the DOM.
    classes: The CSS classes for the widget.
    disabled: Whether the widget is disabled or not.
    clock: An optional clock object (leave as default unless testing).
    gradient: An optional Gradient object (will replace CSS styles in the bar).

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar._validate_total
documentation: Ensure the total is not negative.

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar._compute_percentage
documentation:
Keep the percentage of progress updated automatically.

This will report a percentage of `1` if the total is zero.

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar._watch_progress
documentation: Perform update when progress is modified.

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar._watch_total
documentation: Update when the total is modified.

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.advance
documentation:
Advance the progress of the progress bar by the given amount.

Example:
    ```py
    progress_bar.advance(10)  # Advance 10 steps.
    ```

Args:
    advance: Number of steps to advance progress by.

methods.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.update
documentation:
Update the progress bar with the given options.

Example:
    ```py
    progress_bar.update(
        total=200,  # Set new total to 200 steps.
        progress=50,  # Set the progress to 50 (out of 200).
    )
    ```

Args:
    total: New total number of steps.
    progress: Set the progress to the given number of steps.
    advance: Advance the progress by this number of steps.

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.progress
documentation: The progress so far, in number of steps.

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.total
documentation:
The total number of steps associated with this progress bar, when known.

The value `None` will render an indeterminate progress bar.

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.percentage
documentation:
The percentage of progress that has been completed.

The percentage is a value between 0 and 1 and the returned value is only
`None` if the total progress of the bar hasn't been set yet.

Example:
    ```py
    progress_bar = ProgressBar()
    print(progress_bar.percentage)  # None
    progress_bar.update(total=100)
    progress_bar.advance(50)
    print(progress_bar.percentage)  # 0.5
    ```

class_variables.name: textual.widgets._progress_bar.ProgressBar.gradient
documentation: Optional gradient object (will replace CSS styling in bar).
A progress bar widget.

classes.name: textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog

methods.name: textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog.__init__
documentation:
Create a `RichLog` widget.

Args:
    max_lines: Maximum number of lines in the log or `None` for no maximum.
    min_width: Width to use for calls to `write` with no specified `width`.
    wrap: Enable word wrapping (default is off).
    highlight: Automatically highlight content. By default, the `ReprHighlighter` is used.
        To customize highlighting, set `highlight=True` and then set the `highlighter`
        attribute to an instance of `Highlighter`.
    markup: Apply Rich console markup.
    auto_scroll: Enable automatic scrolling to end.
    name: The name of the text log.
    id: The ID of the text log in the DOM.
    classes: The CSS classes of the text log.
    disabled: Whether the text log is disabled or not.

methods.name: textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog._make_renderable
documentation:
Make content renderable.

Args:
    content: Content to render.

Returns:
    A Rich renderable.

methods.name: textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog.write
documentation:
Write a string or a Rich renderable to the bottom of the log.

Notes:
    The rendering of content will be deferred until the size of the `RichLog` is known.
    This means if you call `write` in `compose` or `on_mount`, the content will not be
    rendered immediately.

Args:
    content: Rich renderable (or a string).
    width: Width to render, or `None` to use `RichLog.min_width`.
        If specified, `expand` and `shrink` will be ignored.
    expand: Permit expanding of content to the width of the content region of the RichLog.
        If `width` is specified, then `expand` will be ignored.
    shrink: Permit shrinking of content to fit within the content region of the RichLog.
        If `width` is specified, then `shrink` will be ignored.
    scroll_end: Enable automatic scroll to end, or `None` to use `self.auto_scroll`.
    animate: Enable animation if the log will scroll.

Returns:
    The `RichLog` instance.

methods.name: textual.widgets._rich_log.RichLog.clear
documentation:
Clear the text log.

Returns:
    The `RichLog` instance.
A widget for logging Rich renderables and text.

documentation:
Construct a new `TextArea`.

Args:

    language: The language to use.
    theme: The theme to use.
    soft_wrap: Enable soft wrapping.
    tab_behavior: If 'focus', pressing tab will switch focus. If 'indent', pressing tab will insert a tab.
    read_only: Enable read-only mode. This prevents edits using the keyboard.
    show_line_numbers: Show line numbers on the left edge.
    line_number_start: What line number to start on.
    max_checkpoints: The maximum number of undo history checkpoints to retain.
    name: The name of the `TextArea` widget.
    id: The ID of the widget, used to refer to it from Textual CSS.
    classes: One or more Textual CSS compatible class names separated by spaces.
    disabled: True if the widget is disabled.
    tooltip: Optional tooltip.

methods.name: textual.widgets._text_area.TextArea.code_editor
documentation:
Construct a new `TextArea` with sensible defaults for editing code.

This instantiates a `TextArea` with line numbers enabled, soft wrapping
disabled, "indent" tab behavior, and the "monokai" theme.

Args:

    language: The language to use.
    theme: The theme to use.
    soft_wrap: Enable soft wrapping.
    tab_behavior: If 'focus', pressing tab will switch focus. If 'indent', pressing tab will insert a tab.
    show_line_numbers: Show line numbers on the left edge.
    line_number_start: What line number to start on.
    name: The name of the `TextArea` widget.
    id: The ID of the widget, used to refer to it from Textual CSS.
    classes: One or more Textual CSS compatible class names separated by spaces.
    disabled: True if the widget is disabled.
    tooltip: Optional tooltip
