    classes: The CSS classes for the screen.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.is_modal
documentation: Is the screen modal?

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.is_current
documentation: Is the screen current (i.e. visible to user)?

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._update_timer
documentation: Timer used to perform updates.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.layers
documentation:
Layers from parent.

Returns:
    Tuple of layer names.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.refresh_bindings
documentation: Call to request a refresh of bindings.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._binding_chain
documentation: Binding chain from this screen.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._modal_binding_chain
documentation: The binding chain, ignoring everything before the last modal.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.active_bindings
documentation:
Get currently active bindings for this screen.

If no widget is focused, then app-level bindings are returned.
If a widget is focused, then any bindings present in the screen and app are merged and returned.

This property may be used to inspect current bindings.

Returns:
    A map of keys to a tuple containing (NAMESPACE, BINDING, ENABLED).

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._arrange
documentation:
Arrange children.

Args:
    size: Size of container.

Returns:
    Widget locations.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.is_active
documentation: Is the screen active (i.e. visible and top of the stack)?

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.render
documentation:
Render method inherited from widget, used to render the screen's background.

Returns:
    Background renderable.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.get_offset
documentation:
Get the absolute offset of a given Widget.

Args:
    widget: A widget

Returns:
    The widget's offset relative to the top left of the terminal.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.get_widget_at
documentation:
Get the widget at a given coordinate.

Args:
    x: X Coordinate.
    y: Y Coordinate.

Returns:
    Widget and screen region.

Raises:
    NoWidget: If there is no widget under the screen coordinate.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.get_widgets_at
documentation:
Get all widgets under a given coordinate.

Args:
    x: X coordinate.
    y: Y coordinate.

Returns:
    Sequence of (WIDGET, REGION) tuples.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.get_focusable_widget_at
documentation:
Get the focusable widget under a given coordinate.

If the widget directly under the given coordinate is not focusable, then this method will check
if any of the ancestors are focusable. If no ancestors are focusable, then `None` will be returned.

Args:
    x: X coordinate.
    y: Y coordinate.

Returns:
    A `Widget`, or `None` if there is no focusable widget underneath the coordinate.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.get_style_at
documentation:
Get the style under a given coordinate.

Args:
    x: X Coordinate.
    y: Y Coordinate.

Returns:
    Rich Style object.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.find_widget
documentation:
Get the screen region of a Widget.

Args:
    widget: A Widget within the composition.

Returns:
    Region relative to screen.

Raises:
    NoWidget: If the widget could not be found in this screen.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.focus_chain
documentation: A list of widgets that may receive focus, in focus order.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._move_focus
documentation:
Move the focus in the given direction.

If no widget is currently focused, this will focus the first focusable widget.
If no focusable widget matches the given CSS selector, focus is set to `None`.

Args:
    direction: 1 to move forward, -1 to move backward, or
        0 to keep the current focus.
    selector: CSS selector to filter
        what nodes can be focused.

Returns:
    Newly focused widget, or None for no focus. If the return
        is not `None`, then it is guaranteed that the widget returned matches
        the CSS selectors given in the argument.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.focus_next
documentation:
Focus the next widget, optionally filtered by a CSS selector.

If no widget is currently focused, this will focus the first focusable widget.
If no focusable widget matches the given CSS selector, focus is set to `None`.

Args:
    selector: CSS selector to filter
        what nodes can be focused.

Returns:
    Newly focused widget, or None for no focus. If the return
        is not `None`, then it is guaranteed that the widget returned matches
        the CSS selectors given in the argument.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.focus_previous
documentation:
Focus the previous widget, optionally filtered by a CSS selector.

If no widget is currently focused, this will focus the first focusable widget.
If no focusable widget matches the given CSS selector, focus is set to `None`.

Args:
    selector: CSS selector to filter
        what nodes can be focused.

Returns:
    Newly focused widget, or None for no focus. If the return
        is not `None`, then it is guaranteed that the widget returned matches
        the CSS selectors given in the argument.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.maximize
documentation:
Maximize a widget, so it fills the screen.

Args:
    widget: Widget to maximize.
    container: If one of the widgets ancestors is a maximizeable widget, maximize that instead.

Returns:
    `True` if the widget was maximized, otherwise `False`.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.minimize
documentation: Restore any maximized widget to normal state.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.action_maximize
documentation: Action to maximize the currently focused widget.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.action_minimize
documentation: Action to minimize the currently maximized widget.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.action_blur
documentation: Action to remove focus (if set).

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._reset_focus
documentation:
Reset the focus when a widget is removed

Args:
    widget: A widget that is removed.
    avoiding: Optional list of nodes to avoid.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._update_focus_styles
documentation:
Update CSS for focus changes.

Args:
    focused: The widget that was focused.
    blurred: The widget that was blurred.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.set_focus
documentation:
Focus (or un-focus) a widget. A focused widget will receive key events first.

Args:
    widget: Widget to focus, or None to un-focus.
    scroll_visible: Scroll widget in to view.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._extend_compose
documentation:
Insert Textual's own internal widgets.

Args:
    widgets: The list of widgets to be composed.

This method adds the tooltip, if required, and also adds the
container for `Toast`s.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._on_mount
documentation: Set up the tooltip-clearing signal when we mount.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._compositor_refresh
documentation: Perform a compositor refresh.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._on_timer_update
documentation: Called by the _update_timer.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._invoke_and_clear_callbacks
documentation:
If there are scheduled callbacks to run, call them and clear
the callback queue.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._invoke_later
documentation:
Enqueue a callback to be invoked after the screen is repainted.

Args:
    callback: A callback.
    sender: The sender (active message pump) of the callback.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._push_result_callback
documentation:
Add a result callback to the screen.

Args:
    requester: The object requesting the callback.
    callback: The callback.
    future: A Future to hold the result.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._pop_result_callback
documentation: Remove the latest result callback from the stack.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._refresh_layout
documentation: Refresh the layout (can change size and positions of widgets).

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._get_inline_height
documentation:
Get the inline height (number of lines to display when running inline mode).

Args:
    size: Size of the terminal

Returns:
    Height for inline mode.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._screen_resized
documentation: Called by App when the screen is resized.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._on_screen_resume
documentation: Screen has resumed.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._on_screen_suspend
documentation: Screen has suspended.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._update_tooltip
documentation: Update the content of the tooltip.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._clear_tooltip
documentation: Unconditionally clear any existing tooltip.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._maybe_clear_tooltip
documentation:
Check if the widget under the mouse cursor still pertains to the tooltip.

If they differ, the tooltip will be removed.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._handle_tooltip_timer
documentation:
Called by a timer from _handle_mouse_move to update the tooltip.

Args:
    widget: The widget under the mouse.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen._translate_mouse_move_event
documentation:
Returns a mouse move event whose relative coordinates are translated to
the origin of the specified region.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.dismiss
documentation:
Dismiss the screen, optionally with a result.

Any callback provided in [push_screen][textual.app.App.push_screen] will be invoked with the supplied result.

Only the active screen may be dismissed. This method will produce a warning in the logs if
called on an inactive screen (but otherwise have no effect).

!!! warning

    Textual will raise a [`ScreenError`][textual.app.ScreenError] if you await the return value from a
    message handler on the Screen being dismissed. If you want to dismiss the current screen, you can
    call `self.dismiss()` _without_ awaiting.

Args:
    result: The optional result to be passed to the result callback.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.pop_until_active
documentation:
Pop any screens on top of this one, until this screen is active.

Raises:
    ScreenError: If this screen is not in the current mode.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.action_dismiss
documentation:
A wrapper around [`dismiss`][textual.screen.Screen.dismiss] that can be called as an action.

Args:
    result: The optional result to be passed to the result callback.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.can_view_entire
documentation:
Check if a given widget is fully within the current screen.

Note: This doesn't necessarily equate to a widget being visible.
There are other reasons why a widget may not be visible.

Args:
    widget: A widget.

Returns:
    `True` if the entire widget is in view, `False` if it is partially visible or not in view.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.can_view_partial
documentation:
Check if a given widget is at least partially within the current view.

Args:
    widget: A widget.

Returns:
    `True` if the any part of the widget is in view, `False` if it is completely outside of the screen.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.validate_title
documentation: Ensure the title is a string or `None`.

methods.name: textual.screen.Screen.validate_sub_title
documentation: Ensure the sub-title is a string or `None`.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.AUTO_FOCUS
documentation:
A selector to determine what to focus automatically when the screen is activated.

The widget focused is the first that matches the given [CSS selector](/guide/queries/#query-selectors).
Set to `None` to inherit the value from the screen's app.
Set to `""` to disable auto focus.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.CSS
documentation:
Inline CSS, useful for quick scripts. Rules here take priority over CSS_PATH.

Note:
    This CSS applies to the whole app.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.CSS_PATH
documentation:
File paths to load CSS from.

Note:
    This CSS applies to the whole app.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.TITLE
documentation:
A class variable to set the *default* title for the screen.

This overrides the app title.
To update the title while the screen is running,
you can set the [title][textual.screen.Screen.title] attribute.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.SUB_TITLE
documentation:
A class variable to set the *default* sub-title for the screen.

This overrides the app sub-title.
To update the sub-title while the screen is running,
you can set the [sub_title][textual.screen.Screen.sub_title] attribute.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.focused
documentation:
The focused [widget][textual.widget.Widget] or `None` for no focus.
To set focus, do not update this value directly. Use [set_focus][textual.screen.Screen.set_focus] instead.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.stack_updates
documentation: An integer that updates when the screen is resumed.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.sub_title
documentation: Screen sub-title to override [the app sub-title][textual.app.App.sub_title].

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.title
documentation: Screen title to override [the app title][textual.app.App.title].

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.COMMANDS
documentation:
Command providers used by the [command palette](/guide/command_palette), associated with the screen.

Should be a set of [`command.Provider`][textual.command.Provider] classes.

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.ALLOW_IN_MAXIMIZED_VIEW
documentation:
A selector for the widgets (direct children of Screen) that are allowed in the maximized view (in addition to maximized widget). Or
`None` to default to [App.ALLOW_IN_MAXIMIZED_VIEW][textual.app.App.ALLOW_IN_MAXIMIZED_VIEW]

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.ESCAPE_TO_MINIMIZE
documentation: Use escape key to minimize (potentially overriding bindings) or `None` to defer to [`App.ESCAPE_TO_MINIMIZE`][textual.app.App.ESCAPE_TO_MINIMIZE].

class_variables.name: textual.screen.Screen.maximized
documentation: The currently maximized widget, or `None` for no maximized widget.
The base class for screens.

classes.name: textual.screen.ModalScreen
documentation:
A screen with bindings that take precedence over the App's key bindings.

The default styling of a modal screen will dim the screen underneath.

classes.name: textual.screen.SystemModalScreen
documentation:
A variant of `ModalScreen` for internal use.

This version of `ModalScreen` allows us to build system-level screens;
the type being used to indicate that the screen should be isolated from
the main application.

Note:
    This screen is set to *not* inherit CSS.

classes.name: textual.renderables.digits.Digits

methods.name: textual.renderables.digits.Digits.render
documentation:
Render with the given style

Args:
    style: Rich Style.

Returns:
    Result of render.

methods.name: textual.renderables.digits.Digits.get_width
documentation:
Calculate the width without rendering.

Args:
    text: Text which may be displayed in the `Digits` widget.

Returns:
    width of the text (in cells).

Renders a 3X3 unicode 'font' for numerical values.

Args:
    text: Text to display.
    style: Style to apply to the digits.

classes.name: textual.renderables.gradient.VerticalGradient
documentation: Draw a vertical gradient.

classes.name: textual.renderables.gradient.LinearGradient
documentation:
Render a linear gradient with a rotation.

Args:
    angle: Angle of rotation in degrees.
    stops: List of stop consisting of pairs of offset (between 0 and 1) and color.
